title: hydrocotyle humboldtii var. isoloba
intro: Hydrocotyle humboldtii var. isoloba, also known as Guayusa or Bolivian water pepper, is a perennial plant native to the Andean region of South America, belonging to the Hydrocotyle genus. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly as an anti-inflammatory and antioxidant agent, due to its high concentration of flavonoids and other polyphenolic compounds. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, including lowering blood pressure, improving cardiovascular health, and reducing the risk of certain cancers. Hydrocotyle humboldtii var. isoloba is typically prepared as a tea or infusion, and it can also be found in capsule or tincture form, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it for medicinal purposes.
